channel.title: NDTV News Search Records Found 1000
channel.description: NDTV.com provides the latest information from and in-depth coverage of India and the world. Find breaking news, India news, top stories, elections, politics, business, cricket, movies, lifestyle, health, live TV, videos, photos and more.

item.title: France Returns 23 Syrian Treasures After 15 Years As Macron Visits Damascus

media:content.media:description: The artifacts were flown on Macron's aircraft and returned to Syria's National Museum.

item.description: The artifacts include Roman bronze objects, Byzantine and Islamic-era pieces and a richly coloured mosaic panel that once adorned the Umayyad Mosque.
item.content:encoded: The artifacts include Roman bronze objects, Byzantine and Islamic-era pieces and a richly coloured mosaic panel that once adorned the Umayyad Mosque.

item.title: Why US Once Exploded A Large Nuclear Bomb In Space

media:content.media:description: Starfish Prime was a US nuclear weapons test carried out on July 9, 1962

item.description: The US carried out the Starfish Prime test by detonating a 1.4-megaton thermonuclear bomb about 400 kilometres above Earth.
item.content:encoded: The US carried out the Starfish Prime test by detonating a 1.4-megaton thermonuclear bomb about 400 kilometres above Earth.

item.title: Rs 4.3 Lakh For Info: How Italian Spies Sold NATO Secrets To Russia

media:content.media:description: Iralian spy met his Russian contact in parks and bars

item.description: The 59-year-old retired spy received Euro 4,000 in cash, the agreed-upon price for each highly classified piece of information, after every exchange.
item.content:encoded: The 59-year-old retired spy received Euro 4,000 in cash, the agreed-upon price for each highly classified piece of information, after every exchange.

item.title: Nigel Farage's Election Gamble Backfires. He Will Now Fight A 'Garbage Can'

media:content.media:description: Nigel Farage's most prominent challengers is Count Binface, a garbage-can wearing comedian

item.description: Nigel Farage resigned as the member of parliament for Clacton-on-Sea in southeast England on Tuesday amid growing controversy over alleged undeclared financial donations.
item.content:encoded: Nigel Farage resigned as the member of parliament for Clacton-on-Sea in southeast England on Tuesday amid growing controversy over alleged undeclared financial donations.

item.title: Engineering Students In Italy Build 65-Foot Paper Plane, Set World Record

media:content.media:description: The group said the project involved months of study, simulations, mistakes, and fresh starts.

item.description: They said they were students who believed that, with the right approach, even a piece of paper could become real engineering.
item.content:encoded: They said they were students who believed that, with the right approach, even a piece of paper could become real engineering.

item.title: Sheikh Hasina To Return To Bangladesh By December, Surrender With Colleagues

media:content.media:description: Bangladesh has repeatedly urged India to extradite Sheikh Hasina.

item.description: Sheikh Hasina fled Bangladesh in 2024 after protests ended her 20 years as prime minister across multiple terms.
item.content:encoded: Sheikh Hasina fled Bangladesh in 2024 after protests ended her 20 years as prime minister across multiple terms.

item.title: China Successfully Tests Rocket Recovery System, Catches Up With Falcon9

media:content.media:description: China's Long March-10B lifted off from the Hainan commercial space launch site.

item.description: The Long March-10B carrier rocket, a reusable liquid-fueled commercial launch vehicle, stands approximately 63 meters tall with a diameter of five meters.
item.content:encoded: The Long March-10B carrier rocket, a reusable liquid-fueled commercial launch vehicle, stands approximately 63 meters tall with a diameter of five meters.

item.title: Australian Influencer Kidnapped Twice In One Day In Italy, Shares Terrifying Ordeal

media:content.media:description: Stephanie is known for sharing DIY home renovation videos on social media.

item.description: An Australian woman holidaying alone in Sicily was kidnapped twice in a single night, first by a reckless driver and then by a security guard who offered her a lift, escaping both times by fleeing on foot.
item.content:encoded: An Australian woman holidaying alone in Sicily was kidnapped twice in a single night, first by a reckless driver and then by a security guard who offered her a lift, escaping both times by fleeing on foot.

item.title: Bill Gates Heir's Shopping App Took Credit For Sales It Didn't Drive

media:content.media:description: Phoebe Gates, daughter of billionaire Bill Gates, has co-founded an advertising startup Phia.

item.description: Phia registered fake clicks on retailers' websites, allowing it to replace another referrer's unique code with its own, according to separate testing by Edelman, Capital One Shopping and Bloomberg.
item.content:encoded: Phia registered fake clicks on retailers' websites, allowing it to replace another referrer's unique code with its own, according to separate testing by Edelman, Capital One Shopping and Bloomberg.

item.title: Mexican Immigrant Killed In ICE Shooting Was Not The Target: US

media:content.media:description: Family members of Lorenzo Salgado Araujo grieve at a makeshift memorial during a candlelight vigil.

item.description: Lorenzo Salgado Araujo was a homebuilder who had lived in the US for more than 35 years, had no criminal record and was close to finishing the process of obtaining legal status.
item.content:encoded: Lorenzo Salgado Araujo was a homebuilder who had lived in the US for more than 35 years, had no criminal record and was close to finishing the process of obtaining legal status.

item.title: Opinion: Opinion | Satluj To Santosh To Padmaavat, How Getting Offended Became India's Favourite Hobby
item.description: There are three reasons why films based on real incidents or beloved myths depart from the truth
item.content:encoded: There are three reasons why films based on real incidents or beloved myths depart from the truth

item.title: Gun With A Name, Bullets And A Waiver: Turkey's Unusual Gift To NATO Leaders

media:content.media:description: Erdogan wanted to showcase Turkey's defence industry with the gift

item.description: It was set in a wooden display box, lined in black, featuring Turkey's flag and the NATO logo as well as a placard inscribed "Gumusay, the first revolver-type handgun produced in our country" in Turkish and English.
item.content:encoded: It was set in a wooden display box, lined in black, featuring Turkey's flag and the NATO logo as well as a placard inscribed "Gumusay, the first revolver-type handgun produced in our country" in Turkish and English.

item.title: After Turkey, Egypt Denies Entry To 2,000-Passenger LGBTQ+ Cruise Ship

media:content.media:description: The ship is operated by Virgin Voyages and chartered by LGBTQ+ travel company Atlantis Events

item.description: Passengers, including Broadway performer Patti LuPone, woke up on Thursday to find a letter slipped under their cabin doors informing them that the ship had been denied entry.
item.content:encoded: Passengers, including Broadway performer Patti LuPone, woke up on Thursday to find a letter slipped under their cabin doors informing them that the ship had been denied entry.

item.title: Watch: Ape Cares For Baby Bird After It Lands In Her Enclosure At US Zoo

media:content.media:description: Zoo keepers said the primate cradled, groomed, and protected the young bird.

item.description: A siamang named Eve at ABQ BioPark Zoo gently cared for a baby robin that accidentally entered her enclosure.
item.content:encoded: A siamang named Eve at ABQ BioPark Zoo gently cared for a baby robin that accidentally entered her enclosure.

item.title: Video: Thai PM Plays Saxophone, Malaysian PM Performs "My Way" During Official Lunch

media:content.media:description: Thailand's Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul with his Malaysian counterpart Anwar Ibrahim

item.description: The performance took place during Charnvirakul Anutin's two-day official visit to Malaysia, where he held bilateral talks on ways to strengthen ties between their countries.
item.content:encoded: The performance took place during Charnvirakul Anutin's two-day official visit to Malaysia, where he held bilateral talks on ways to strengthen ties between their countries.

item.title: Goat Turns Unexpected Guide, Helps Firefighters Find Route To Wildfire In US

media:content.media:description: He later learned on Thursday that the goat's name was Goldie.

item.description: The Rock Creek Canyon fire broke out early on Wednesday in an area west of Cheyenne Mountain State Park.
item.content:encoded: The Rock Creek Canyon fire broke out early on Wednesday in an area west of Cheyenne Mountain State Park.

item.title: Attacks On Salman Khan, Siddhu Moosewala: Inside US' Charges Against Lawrence Bishnoi

media:content.media:description: The US probe found that Bishnoi's gang evolved into a 'transnational criminal syndicate'.

item.description: The US probe found that Bishnoi continued directing his associates across the globe personally using contraband cellphones and other voice-over-internet-protocol devices smuggled into his jail cell.
item.content:encoded: The US probe found that Bishnoi continued directing his associates across the globe personally using contraband cellphones and other voice-over-internet-protocol devices smuggled into his jail cell.

item.title: "US Bases Our Problem, Not Neighbours": Iranian Experts On Middle East Attacks

media:content.media:description: Iranian experts said that Khamenei's death in the US-Israel strikes has "united the people of Iran".

item.description: Professor Seyed Emamain, Founder of Governance and Policy Thinktank (GPTT), and Ali Salehian, Head of the International Department GPTT, spoke to NDTV about the mass uprising against Ali Khamenei's rule in January to mass support after his death.
item.content:encoded: Professor Seyed Emamain, Founder of Governance and Policy Thinktank (GPTT), and Ali Salehian, Head of the International Department GPTT, spoke to NDTV about the mass uprising against Ali Khamenei's rule in January to mass support after his death.

item.title: Explosions Heard In Iran As Ali Khamenei Is Buried, US Denies Strikes

media:content.media:description: Several areas in Bushehr province were targeted, an official said.

item.description: A US-Israeli projectile reportedly hit a military headquarters on the outskirts of Bushehr, home to Tehran's only civilian nuclear plant, on Thursday.
item.content:encoded: A US-Israeli projectile reportedly hit a military headquarters on the outskirts of Bushehr, home to Tehran's only civilian nuclear plant, on Thursday.

item.title: How Israel Used 'Kill-Plot' Intel To Convince Trump To Attack Iran Again

media:content.media:description: On Thursday, Trump and Netanyahu spoke again

item.description: Tehran, for years, has openly threatened to retaliate against Trump for America's alleged role in the assassination of Qassem Soleimani, who was a top general of the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps during the president's first term.
item.content:encoded: Tehran, for years, has openly threatened to retaliate against Trump for America's alleged role in the assassination of Qassem Soleimani, who was a top general of the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps during the president's first term.
